display.purpose: Hero and marketing headlines only. Inter Tight. Never in product chrome.

headline.purpose: Largest in-product title and marketing h1. Inter Tight.

heading.purpose: Preferences section headers. Switches to the system font.

panel-title.purpose: NSPanel and window title weight; the prompt question and list-pane title.

body.purpose: Default body and button label, mac-native 13px. Prose caps at 65-75ch.

body-prominent.purpose: A step up for reading-weight content.

label.purpose: Functional uppercase header: sidebar sections, field labels, the app-name eyebrow. Not a decorative section kicker.

mono.purpose: Timecodes, durations, paths, identifiers, counts, and key caps. JetBrains Mono.

shadows.name: hairline
shadows.purpose: The Retina divider for internal table rows and surface edges. The default separator, not a shadow.

shadows.name: resting-xs
shadows.purpose: A barely-there lift for a single raised control.

shadows.name: raised-sm
shadows.purpose: Sheets and panels inside a window.

shadows.name: hud-float
shadows.purpose: Only the meeting prompt and floating panels, always paired with the hud fill and backdrop blur.

motion.name: ease-out
motion.purpose: Apple's default-ish out curve. No overshoot, no spring. The standard easing for state transitions.

motion.name: dur-base
motion.purpose: Default transition; matches the 0.18s panel fade in the Swift code.

motion.name: dur-slow
motion.purpose: The ceiling. Recording-dot opacity loop runs at 1.6s; nothing else is slower than base by much.

components.name: Primary Button
components.description: The one payoff action per surface: Record, Save, Publish, Use candidate.

components.name: Secondary Button
components.description: The non-payoff action: Skip, Cancel, Always for Zoom, Keep current.

components.name: Ghost Button
components.description: Toolbar and low-emphasis actions; no border at rest.

components.name: Text Input
components.description: Settings field. Focus shifts the border to teal and adds the teal ring; path fields use mono.

components.name: Toggle (on)
components.description: Binary setting. Ash off, Live-Signal Teal on; the knob slides on a 180ms ease-out.

components.name: Status Pill (recording)
components.description: The workhorse state component. Tinted background, matching border and text, a dot or icon plus a label. The recording dot pulses on a 1.6s opacity loop, never a scale pulse.

components.name: Workflow Chip
components.description: A meeting's workflow identity across the whole surface: a curated tonal dot (teal, deep-teal, amber, or ink, never coral) plus the name.

components.name: Kbd Key
components.description: A key-binding cap. Mono, paper-raised, with a 1px hairline and a subtle bottom light.

components.name: Scope Row (active)
components.description: An active smart-folder row in the rail: a full Live-Signal Teal fill with white text and an inverted mono count.
components.html: All meetings42

components.name: Meeting Row
components.description: The signature Library list row: app glyph, title over a meta line (source, duration, workflow chip), a status pill, and a right-aligned mono timestamp. Selection is a Signal Wash background, never a side-stripe.
components.html: Release 3.2 validation reviewZoom · 47:21ReadyToday 11:02
components.css: .ds-row{display:flex;align-items:center;gap:10px;height:46px;padding:0 14px;cursor:pointer}.ds-row:hover{background:#F5F6F8}.ds-row-selected,.ds-row-selected:hover{background:#DCF1EF}.ds-row-glyph{width:22px;height:22px;display:flex;align-items:center;justify-content:center;color:#6E747F;flex-shrink:0}.ds-row-main{display:flex;flex-direction:column;gap:1px;flex:1;min-width:0}.ds-row-title{font:500 13px/1.2 -apple-system,'SF Pro Text',Inter,sans-serif;color:#14161A;white-space:nowrap;overflow:hidden;text-overflow:ellipsis}.ds-row-meta{display:flex;align-items:center;gap:6px;font:400 11px/1.2 -apple-system,'SF Pro Text',Inter,sans-serif;color:#6E747F}.ds-row-meta .ds-sep{color:#9098A4}.ds-row-meta .ds-mono{font-family:'JetBrains Mono','SF Mono',ui-monospace,monospace}.ds-pill-ok{display:inline-flex;align-items:center;gap:5px;height:19px;padding:0 8px;border-radius:999px;font:500 11px/1 -apple-system,'SF Pro Text',Inter,sans-serif;color:#1F8F4E;border:0.5px solid rgba(31,143,78,0.25)}.ds-row-time{min-width:92px;text-align:right;font:400 11px/1 'JetBrains Mono','SF Mono',ui-monospace,monospace;color:#4A4F58;white-space:nowrap}

narrative.overview:
The system serves one invariant that outlasts every surface: a complete, local, trustworthy record of your meetings, kept on your own Mac, that you capture, read, search, and reason over. meeting-pipe is a menu-bar utility first, seen at only four moments (the prompt when a call is detected, the recording HUD, the done-notification, the Library), but the design is built so the record it keeps can be browsed, re-published, edited, and one day analyzed for patterns without inventing a new visual language each time. Whatever gets added stays in one register: local-first, quiet, deliberate.

Three motifs carry the whole thing. Paper: the canvas reads as a calm, barely-warm document where transcripts and summaries live, something you would want to read, not a chrome panel. Hairlines, not shadows: mac-native chrome separates surfaces with 1px and 0.5px borders, reserving shadow for the few surfaces that genuinely float. One signal: a single electric teal that means capture-in-progress and earns its place by rarity, never a decorative gradient or a fill on a large surface. Density is tight and mac-native throughout: a 13px body on a compact 1.125 ramp, a 4px spacing grid living mostly on the 8 / 12 / 16 / 20 ticks, fixed window sizes. This is interface, not a content site.

The system explicitly rejects three families: the SaaS marketing cliche (gradients, sparkle, decorative AI iconography, emoji, celebration toasts), the generic Electron-app feel (web-rendered chrome pretending to be a desktop app), and the modern dark-glass fintech and AI-tool family (glassy floating cards, neon accents, gradient borders, futuristic framing). The AI is used under the hood and acknowledged in plain grey text; it is never branded and never celebrated.
keyCharacteristics: Local-first as a visual commitment: the on-device option always renders at the same weight as the cloud one, never tucked into Advanced.
Quiet by default: literal state labels, no celebration, the summary just appears.
Warm ink on paper, never pure black on pure white.
One signal-teal accent, held to roughly 10% of any surface.
Hairlines over shadows; a shadow means this floats.
Fixed window sizes and mac-native density; SF Pro at 13px is canonical.

rules.name: The One Signal Rule
rules.body: Live-Signal Teal appears on no more than about 10% of any surface. It marks one family of things: capture, the active path, the primary action. Its rarity is the point. Decorative teal is forbidden.

rules.name: The Coral-Is-Recording Rule
rules.body: On-Air Coral is the recording dot and the destructive confirm, nothing else. If coral is tinting a button or a background, it is wrong.

rules.name: The No-System-Blue Rule
rules.body: The accent is teal, end-state; macOS system blue is never the accent. The shipped --mp-focus-ring token is still blue and is mid-migration, so until it is recolored, derive teal focus rings inline with color-mix(in srgb, var(--mp-signal-600) 32%, transparent).

rules.name: The Same-Level Rule
rules.body: Wherever the user routes data, the local option renders at the same visual weight as the cloud option. Local-first is shown, not hidden.

rules.name: The Thirteen-Pixel Rule
rules.body: Body and chrome default to 13px, mac-native, on a compact 1.125 ramp. This is interface, not a content reader. Marketing surfaces may climb to display sizes; product chrome never inflates.

rules.name: The No-Bold Rule
rules.body: Weights stop at semibold (600); regular (400) and medium (500) carry the rest. Bold (700 and up) is not in the system, per Apple convention.

rules.name: The System-Font-Is-Canonical Rule
rules.body: The -apple-system and SF Pro stack leads every UI surface. Inter Tight appears only at 28px and above.

rules.name: The Hairlines-Not-Shadows Rule
rules.body: Surfaces at rest separate with 1px or 0.5px borders, never a drop shadow. If a resting card has a shadow, remove it.

rules.name: The Float-Earns-Blur Rule
rules.body: Translucency and backdrop blur belong only to surfaces that float over the desktop (the HUD, the menu-bar dropdown). Cards inside windows are fully opaque. Never double-blur.

dos: Do hold Live-Signal Teal (#0E8C82) to about 10% of any surface: primary action, current selection, active tab, playhead, capture indicator. Use Signal Wash (#DCF1EF) for selected-row tints.
Do separate resting surfaces with hairlines (1px at 10%, 0.5px Retina dividers); reserve shadow for surfaces that float.
Do keep On-Air Coral (#E5484D) for the recording dot and the destructive confirm only.
Do default to SF Pro at 13px on a compact ramp, and stop weights at semibold (600).
Do label every state with text or an icon, not color alone; both light and dark must be color-blind safe.
Do acknowledge the AI in plain grey provenance text (Summarized by Claude Opus 4.8, cloud), at the same weight as the on-device line.
Do keep fixed window sizes and mac-native density (prompt 600x64, preferences 480x560).
Do use the uppercase label only as a functional header (sidebar sections, field labels), never as decoration.
Do honor prefers-reduced-motion: the recording-dot loop, the panel fades, and the tint transitions degrade to crossfades or instant changes.
donts: Don't ship the SaaS marketing cliche: no gradients, no powered-by-Claude sparkle, no decorative AI iconography, no emoji in microcopy, no celebration toasts. Not the Notion AI, ChatGPT, or Perplexity surface aesthetic.
Don't produce a generic Electron-app feel: web-rendered chrome pretending to be a desktop app, the wrong density, the wrong fonts, no SF Symbols. Not Slack, not Discord, not Linear's desktop wrapper.
Don't reach for the modern dark-glass fintech and AI-tool family: glassy floating cards, neon accents, gradient borders, futuristic framing. Wrong even when well-crafted, for a calm menu-bar utility.
Don't use macOS system blue as the accent; the accent is teal, end-state.
Don't use a side-stripe (a border-left or border-right over 1px as a colored accent) on rows, cards, callouts, or alerts. Use a full hairline, a wash tint, or a leading icon instead.
Don't use gradient text (background-clip: text on a gradient). Emphasis is weight or size, in a single solid color.
Don't use glassmorphism by default; blur belongs only to surfaces that float over the desktop, and cards inside windows stay fully opaque (never double-blur).
Don't use pure black (#000) or stack pure white on pure white; ink is Warm Graphite (#14161A) on Soft Document White (#FBFAF7).
Don't add inner shadows, scale or bounce pulses, skeleton shimmer, or decorative motion; motion conveys state only, at 120 to 280ms with no overshoot.

Don't use em-dashes as punctuation in chrome or copy; use commas, hyphens, or rewrite. Reserve the ellipsis for an in-flight state (Processing...) or an action that opens another surface (Preferences...).
Don't let a heading stretch past its container; product windows are fixed-size, so test the real copy at the real width.
